description_long: This is a heavy, mounted automatic rifle resembling a rotary or Gatling-style gun, equipped with a prominent cylindrical barrel assembly and a robust, angular shield for operator protection. The weapon is supported by a sturdy bipod or tripod base, ensuring stability during operation. The rear features a pistol grip and trigger mechanism, while the central section houses a visible rotary drum or feed system for ammunition. The shield is thick and angular, providing frontal coverage, and the barrel is long and reinforced, likely for sustained fire. The overall construction is metallic, with a dark, matte finish and signs of wear, suggesting use in rugged urban environments.
description: A heavy, mounted rotary rifle with a large protective shield, bipod base, and visible drum feed system, finished in dark, worn metal.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the rifle, the rotary drum, pistol grip, and the angular shield.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the circular barrel opening and the full width of the protective shield.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the rotary drum, grip, and the bipod structure supporting the rifle.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the grip, drum feed, and the back of the shield, with the bipod legs visible.
